rules.name: Lantern & Blade
rules.author: Lantern & Blade Project
rules.description: A d20 ruleset for heroic dungeon play: six ability scores, five classes, scaling proficiency, ascending AC, advantage and disadvantage, and the full palette of damage types. Tuned for solo text-first sessions where the GM does most of the lifting and the dice pick up the rest.
rules.design_philosophy: Heroes with a real chance — but not a guaranteed one. Classes matter, choices matter, and the dice decide the middle ground. The GM is patient, concrete, and fair. Combat is tense but survivable; attrition across a dungeon is where the real risk lives.

fighter.name: Fighter
fighter.description: Disciplined martial, trained across arms and armor. Fighters hold lines, open doors, and finish what lighter classes start.

rogue.name: Rogue
rogue.description: Scout, cutpurse, trap-hunter. Rogues win fights by not being where the blow lands, and by knowing exactly where theirs should.

wizard.name: Wizard
wizard.description: Scholar of written magic. Wizards prepare a handful of spells from a grimoire each day, spend them carefully, and rest to prepare anew.

cleric.name: Cleric
cleric.description: Sworn servant of a power. Clerics channel borrowed strength — light, warding, healing — and pay for it with patience, ritual, and obligation.

ranger.name: Ranger
ranger.description: Border-walker. Rangers read terrain and quarry the way others read faces, and they carry minor wilderness magics inherited from older traditions.

damage_types.name: Slashing
damage_types.narrative_cue: blades parting flesh, long clean lines and deep bleeding cuts

damage_types.name: Piercing
damage_types.narrative_cue: narrow punctures, arrows buried, fangs driven in

damage_types.name: Bludgeoning
damage_types.narrative_cue: crushing impact, broken bone, the dull ringing of a well-landed mace

damage_types.name: Fire
damage_types.narrative_cue: searing heat, smoke and charred cloth, flesh that hisses

damage_types.name: Cold
damage_types.narrative_cue: deep frost, numb limbs, breath crystallizing

damage_types.name: Lightning
damage_types.narrative_cue: a white crack, hair standing, the smell of ozone

damage_types.name: Thunder
damage_types.narrative_cue: concussive air, ringing ears, footing lost

damage_types.name: Acid
damage_types.narrative_cue: pitting, dissolving, the reek of eaten metal and cloth

damage_types.name: Poison
damage_types.narrative_cue: cold sweat, weakness, the slow climb of something wrong through the body

damage_types.name: Necrotic
damage_types.narrative_cue: graying flesh, the draining of warmth and will, wounds that refuse to close

damage_types.name: Radiant
damage_types.narrative_cue: unbearable light, the burn of something holy on something it was not meant to touch

damage_types.name: Psychic
damage_types.narrative_cue: pressure behind the eyes, thoughts breaking like thin ice, nosebleeds

damage_types.name: Force
damage_types.narrative_cue: raw pushing magic, bones shaken, unseen strikes leaving bruises in perfect shapes

conditions.name: Blinded
conditions.description: Sight is gone — darkness, smoke, spell, or wound.
conditions.effect: Automatic failure on checks that require sight. Attack rolls against you have advantage; your attack rolls have disadvantage.
conditions.removal: Until the cause ends (smoke clears, spell expires, wound treated).

conditions.name: Charmed
conditions.description: You regard another creature as a friendly acquaintance, even against your better judgment.
conditions.effect: Cannot attack the charmer or target them with harmful effects. The charmer has advantage on social checks against you.
conditions.removal: The effect's duration ends, the charmer harms you, or the source is destroyed.

conditions.name: Frightened
conditions.effect: Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks while the source is in sight. You cannot willingly move closer to the source.
conditions.removal: Source leaves sight for a full round, effect expires, or a successful Wisdom save at the end of each of your turns.

conditions.name: Grappled
conditions.description: Seized — by hands, jaws, or magic.
conditions.effect: Speed becomes 0. Ends if the grappler is incapacitated or removed from reach.
conditions.removal: Successful Strength (Athletics) or Dexterity (Acrobatics) check vs. the grappler's check.

conditions.name: Poisoned
conditions.description: A toxin is working through you.
conditions.effect: Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.
conditions.removal: Until the duration ends, antitoxin is administered, or the poison is neutralized by magic or long rest.

conditions.name: Prone
conditions.description: On the ground — knocked, tripped, or dropped.
conditions.effect: Disadvantage on your attack rolls. Melee attacks against you have advantage; ranged attacks against you have disadvantage.
conditions.removal: Spend half your movement to stand up.

conditions.name: Restrained
conditions.description: Movement bound — by web, rope, root, or spell.
conditions.effect: Speed becomes 0. Disadvantage on attack rolls and Dexterity saves. Attacks against you have advantage.
conditions.removal: Escape via the same check that broke the binding (Athletics/Acrobatics), or the source is removed.

conditions.name: Stunned
conditions.description: Senses overloaded — a blow too clean, a spell too sharp.
conditions.effect: Incapacitated. Cannot move. Speaks only falteringly. Automatically fails Strength and Dexterity saves. Attacks against you have advantage.
conditions.removal: Constitution save at end of each of your turns, or until effect duration ends.

conditions.name: Unconscious
conditions.description: Down — at zero hit points, or asleep, or worse.
conditions.effect: Incapacitated, prone, and unaware. Fails Strength and Dexterity saves. Attacks against you have advantage; melee hits within 5 feet are automatically critical.
conditions.removal: Healing (even 1 HP), a successful Medicine check to stabilize, or the GM's narrative rescue.

conditions.name: Wounded
conditions.description: A lingering injury that has not yet been treated — below half hit points, or carrying a nasty wound from a previous fight.
conditions.effect: Narrative only — the character moves stiffly, bleeds at inconvenient moments, sleeps poorly. GM may call for occasional Constitution checks during exertion.
conditions.removal: Full long rest, a Medicine check with proper supplies, or healing magic.

difficulty.auto_success: Trivial tasks with no consequence, or tasks the character is plainly trained for under calm conditions.
difficulty.auto_failure: Genuinely impossible tasks, or tasks the character cannot attempt without a resource they lack (no torches to search darkness, no tools to disarm).
